title: Permission denied when signing image
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I get this error message when i'm trying to push and sign an image. "Error: creating signature: Permission denied". Not sure if this is a "local" problem (like writing to wrong folders) or if it´s related to the repository.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Login as rootless
2. podman pull docker.io/alpine
3. podman tag docker.io/alpine myrepo/myimage:latest
4. podman login myrepo -u myuser -p mypass
5.  podman push --tls-verify=false --log-level=debug --authfile=$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/containers/auth.json --sign-by=mymail@mycompany.se myrepo/myimage:latest

**Describe the results you received:**

last rows from debug:

...
Signing manifest
DEBU[0001] Looking up image "myrepo/testimage:v0.12" in local containers storage
DEBU[0001] Trying "myrepo/testimage:v0.12" ...
DEBU[0001] parsed reference into "[overlay@/home/myusername/.local/share/containers/storage+/run/user/myuserid/containers:overlay.mount_program=/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs,overlay.mount_program=/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs]@c059bfaa849c4d8e4aecaeb3a10c2d9b3d85f5165c66ad3a4d937758128c4d18"
DEBU[0001] Found image "myrepo/testimage:v0.12" as "myrepo/testimage:v0.12" in local containers storage
Error: creating signature: Permission denied

**Describe the results you expected:**

Files should be written to the sigstore folder? This folder is empty.
Image is beeing pushed to repo but i guess it's not signed correctly.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

cat .config/containers/registries.d/default.yaml
```
default-docker:
  #sigstore: http://myrepo
  sigstore-staging: file:///home/myusername/.local/share/containers/sigstore
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.3.1
API Version:  3.3.1
Go Version:   go1.16.7
Built:        Tue Sep 21 10:41:42 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-3.3.1-9.module+el8.5.0+12697+018f24d7.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**

Not possible, only allowed to use official RHEL packages

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Virtual linux machine, on prem
SELinux=Enforcing (same error in permissive-mode)

comments.body: This might be podman image sign trying to write to a system directory?

This is not writing/uploading signatures, this really is a failure _creating_ the signature. It looks like a failure somewhere in/below GPGMe . Running the command under `strace -ff`, or maybe using the `GPGME_DEBUG` variable might reveal more. (I didn’t try reproducing it now.)

> Not possible, only allowed to use official RHEL packages

Consider also contacting official RH support if you are a customer.

Thanks @rhatdan and @mtrmac! This was not an issue with podman. I believe this error occured when GPG tries to open the password prompt which was not permitted. I solved it by adding "allow-loopback-pinentry" to gpg-agent.conf.
